displayName: VS Code / Copilot Chat (local) remote HTTP MCP
knownQuirks: Configuration uses a top-level `servers` object (not `mcpServers`) and supports optional `oauth.clientId`.
Local Copilot Chat only: the GitHub Copilot cloud agent / code review surface does not support remote OAuth MCP and must never be verified under this spec.
No install deep link may be published until redirect behavior is verified at the pinned version (review restraint).

steps.name: Configure the workspace server in the VS Code `servers` shape
steps.instructions: On a clean VS Code + Copilot Chat install at exact pinned versions (record both in clientVersion), add the protected workspace server using the documented `servers` config shape. Exercise both DCR and, if applicable, a configured oauth.clientId. Fail if any credential, connection URL, or fragment must be placed in the config file or appears in chat.

steps.name: Complete OAuth with DCR or configured client ID
steps.instructions: Let VS Code run OAuth discovery, registration (DCR) or the preregistered client ID, S256 PKCE, exchange, and resource binding; verify the redirect behavior it actually uses. Approve consent as the workspace owner. Fail on any second request for the connection link or any token/authorization URL reflected into chat.

steps.name: One-session lifecycle: initialize through later tool calls
steps.instructions: Verify initialize -> notifications/initialized -> tools/list -> claim -> context -> verify -> ping -> tools/list on ONE MCP session with no undocumented reconnect. Prefer the one-call rateloop_connect_workspace path; also confirm the granular fallback. Repeat the claim to confirm idempotency with exactly one connected event.

steps.name: Agent context read returns the bound policy
steps.instructions: Call rateloop_get_agent_context from Copilot Chat and confirm it returns the effective server-bound human-review configuration for the connected integration only. Record whether the host surfaces server instructions and tool titles. Fail if it exposes private artifacts or another integration's scope.

steps.name: Connection verification is idempotent and non-evaluative
steps.instructions: Call rateloop_verify_connection and confirm success, then repeat it and confirm idempotency. Verify server-side that verification created no review opportunity, evidence, spending, or publishing state, and that exactly one connected event exists.

steps.name: Resume after window reload and new chat without re-asking for the link
steps.instructions: Reload the VS Code window and start a new chat. The agent must rediscover the connection via rateloop_get_agent_context without asking for the connection URL again and without creating a polling service. Fail if a still-valid token family requires a fresh OAuth grant.
